# CollX

## For future Claude
CollX is a SlabMetrics competitor positioned at the hobbyist/community end of the market — large free user base acquired via photo scanning, with a peer-to-peer marketplace as the engagement loop. Relevant when: assessing the hobbyist-conversion opportunity (Phase 2 in the business plan), understanding the free-tier WTP anchoring problem, or evaluating whether CollX's AI feature is a signal that AI-first features are expected.
Key fact: CollX's free tier anchors user WTP at $0 — this is explicitly cited as a monetization weakness and a reason SlabMetrics should price at or above Card Ladder, not compete on price.

## TL;DR

CollX is a free sports card scanner and peer-to-peer marketplace with an optional Pro subscription. Large user base, hobbyist framing, no investment analytics. Pro: $9.99/mo or $99.99/yr. `[Certain]` — [[sources/collx-pro-features]]

## Core Features

**Free tier:**
- Photo-based card identification and instant pricing
- Collection tracking (~500 item cap)
- Peer-to-peer marketplace (buy/sell/trade)
- Social feed and community

**Pro adds:**
- 500 AI-enhanced Scan+ credits/month
- Unlimited collections
- $10/mo marketplace credit
- CSV export
- CollX AI (hobby Q&A assistant)
- Bulk pricing ("Easy Price")
- Group chats, featured listings, priority support
- iOS family sharing
- 3-month free Card Dealer Pro (desktop multi-marketplace listing tool)

`[Certain]` — [[sources/collx-pro-features]]

## Pricing

| Plan | Price |
|---|---|
| Free | $0 |
| Pro Monthly | $9.99/mo |
| Pro Annual | $99.99/yr |

`[Certain]` — [[sources/collx-pro-features]]

## Competitive Assessment (vs. SlabMetrics)

**Their advantage**: Massive free user base from scan-to-price hook. Photo recognition lowers the barrier to entry dramatically. Marketplace creates organic engagement and network effects.

**Their weakness**: No investment analytics, no market indexes, no price alerts, no signal generation, no decision synthesis. Free tier anchors WTP at $0 — converting these users to paid investor tools requires a framing shift, not just feature additions. `[Certain]` — [[sources/collx-pro-features]]

**Relevance to SlabMetrics Phase 2**: The business plan gates the "hobbyist conversion" play (CollX-adjacent audience) until ≥50 paying investor users are retained. CollX's user base is the acquisition pool for that phase — not a v1 competitor. `[Certain]` — [[company/business-plan]]
